title: Adjacent-rival undercut pressure features
summary: F1 pit timing is often triggered by the car immediately ahead or behind, not only by a driver's own tyre age.
rationale: F1 pit timing is often triggered by the car immediately ahead or behind, not only by a driver's own tyre age. Public strategy analyses emphasize undercut/overcut windows, clear air, traffic, and being within striking range of rivals.
implementation_hint: For each Race_Year-LapNumber, join same-lap features from Position-1 and Position+1: neighbor tyre age, compound, stint, PitStop, lap-time residual, and position-change signal. Add relative terms such as older_than_car_ahead, fresher_than_car_behind, neighbor_just_pitted, and pace_delta_to_adjacent_position. Keep the join same-lap/current-lap only, with no next-row target leakage.
expected_effect: Adds strategy-pressure signal where a driver pits next lap because a rival has just stopped, is about to undercut, or has created a pit window.
risk: Without true time gaps, Position adjacency is a coarse proxy; noisy race-lap joins may hurt if sorting or duplicate positions are mishandled.
